src-2010.stepDescription: Set es-MX, install latest Bloom as standard user.
checklistSteps: Sign in with a standard user account.
stepNotes: Verify the latest version installs without admin privileges.

src-2005.stepDescription: Set es-MX, disable feedback, test clean-machine install.
checklistSteps: Set the FEEDBACK environment variable to FALSE.
Run a new-machine install, ideally on a VM.
stepNotes: Refer to BL-8947.

src-2020.stepDescription: Set es-MX, install on clean machine, run smoke test.
checklistSteps: Install the latest Bloom version on a clean machine or VM.
stepNotes: Verify the latest version installs cleanly.

src-2021.stepDescription: Set es-MX and perform the TC2 scenario.
stepNotes: The source row did not include explicit test steps beyond the culture setup.

src-2030.stepDescription: Set es-MX, install latest Bloom with UAC on admin account.
checklistSteps: Sign in with an admin account.
Install the latest Bloom version with User Account Control enabled.
stepNotes: Verify the install completes under UAC.

src-r12.stepDescription: Run update tests and keep update dialog visible.
checklistSteps: Watch the updates or applying updates window while other windows are open.
stepNotes: Verify the update window does not get lost behind other windows.

src-2040.stepDescription: Check for new version and confirm Bloom is current.
checklistSteps: In Help, choose Check for New Version.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom reports that your copy is up to date.

src-2050.stepDescription: Go offline, check updates, confirm recovery after disconnect.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom shows the proper offline response and does not get stuck after losing Internet access.

src-tc105-steps-11-to-22-r15.stepDescription: Reconnect after offline update check and clear stale warning.
checklistSteps: Reconnect to the Internet after an offline check-for-update attempt.
stepNotes: Verify the "Can't check for new version offline" message does not continue after reconnect.

src-2055.stepDescription: Install older Bloom, update it, avoid full installer download.
checklistSteps: Update Bloom to the current version.
stepNotes: Verify the update succeeds without downloading something the size of the full installer.

src-2060.stepDescription: Set es-MX, enable automatic updates, start update.
stepNotes: Verify the update runs automatically if that option is set.

src-r18.stepDescription: Set es-MX, disable automatic updates, start update, approve prompt.
stepNotes: Verify the update waits for user approval before continuing.

src-r19.stepDescription: Set es-MX, update Bloom, confirm registration remains.
stepNotes: Verify registration info is not lost after the update.

src-2070.stepDescription: Set es-MX, update Bloom, check Blorg sign-in.
checklistSteps: Check the Blorg login credentials after the update.
stepNotes: This is currently a bug the team is willing to live with.

src-2080.stepDescription: Set es-MX, check for new version, wait for availability notice.
checklistSteps: Wait for the update check to complete.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom reports that a new version is available, usually after 3-4 minutes.

src-2090.stepDescription: Set es-MX, inspect shortcuts, confirm automatic-download default.
stepNotes: Verify the suggestion defaults to automatic download.

src-2100.stepDescription: Set es-MX, install oldest version, update to current.
checklistSteps: Install the oldest Bloom version you can find.
Run the updater until Bloom reaches the current version.
stepNotes: Verify it updates successfully. BL-15577 suggests squirrel-to-Velopack updates may fail.

src-2110.stepDescription: Install Bloom, block folder access, run, expect troubleshooting dialog.
checklistSteps: Make the Bloom program folder unreadable to everyone.
stepNotes: Expect a failure dialog with a button to the Installation Troubleshooting HowTo page.

checklistSteps: Start an allUsers install from a run-as-admin prompt.
stepNotes: Verify the allUsers install works. Bloom 6.3 no longer offers this option.

src-2130.stepDescription: Try allUsers install without admin, check warning.
checklistSteps: Start an allUsers install without run-as-admin.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom shows the expected admin-required message. Bloom 6.3 no longer offers this option.

src-2140.stepDescription: Check updates from allUsers install and review admin-managed message.
checklistSteps: Use an earlier allUsers Bloom install.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom says your system administrator manages updates. BL-12305 is tolerated; Bloom 6.3 may make this obsolete.

src-tc7-r28.stepDescription: Prepare Windows VM, snapshot it, install Bloom, confirm .NET upgrade.
checklistSteps: Take a VM snapshot so you can restore quickly.
Set up the Windows versions you want to test.
stepNotes: Ensure the required .NET version is upgraded if the VM's version is inadequate.

src-tc357-r29.stepDescription: Start Bloom 6.3 on Windows 10, check unsupported behavior.
stepNotes: Verify Windows 10 is not supported starting in 6.3. Test BL-15248.

src-tc358-r30.stepDescription: Start Bloom on Windows 7, confirm unsupported behavior.
stepNotes: Verify Windows 7 is no longer supported.

src-tc8-r33.stepDescription: Open book, delete .htm, restart Bloom, verify recovery.
checklistSteps: Delete the .htm file, but leave bookhtml.bak in place.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom recovers, shows a warning, and restores from a recent backup.

src-2170.stepDescription: Open book, delete .htm and backup, restart without crash.
checklistSteps: Delete the .htm file and the bookhtml.bak file.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom does not crash.

src-tc10-r35.stepDescription: Zip collection, delete folder, open zipped .bloomcollection without extracting.
checklistSteps: Zip up any Bloom collection folder.
Open the zip file without extracting it.
Attempt to open a .bloomcollection file from inside the zip.
stepNotes: The message should say: "It looks like you are trying to open a Bloom Collection from inside of a ZIP file. You need to first unzip the ZIP file, and then open the collection."

src-tc110-steps-1-to-7-r42.stepDescription: Open Report a Problem and send a test report.
checklistSteps: In Bloom Get Help, click "Report a Problem".
stepNotes: Verify the email is sent to the address on the form, the book is attached as a zip file, and the screenshot is correct.

src-tc110-step-8-to-11-r43.stepDescription: Open report and confirm reporter details are pre-filled.
checklistSteps: Open the report from the previous step.
stepNotes: Verify your registration email and name are filled in.

src-tc110-step-12-to-16-r44.stepDescription: Reply in YouTrack with @reporter and confirm email delivery.
stepNotes: Verify the reporter's email receives the response.

src-tc110-step-17-to-20-r45.stepDescription: Reply by email to Bloom Team and verify it appears in YouTrack.
checklistSteps: In the reporter's email, send a response back to the Bloom Team.
stepNotes: Verify the response shows up in YouTrack, allowing for a delay of up to about 10 minutes.

src-tc93-r46.stepDescription: Report a problem, open Learn More, verify no close button.
stepNotes: Verify there is no close button.

src-tc14-r47.stepDescription: Prepare clean machine, register older Bloom, upgrade, keep registration.
checklistSteps: Use a fresh machine or VM, or uninstall Bloom and remove its local remnants.
Go to C:/Users/<name>/AppData/Local/SIL/Bloom and remove directories that start with 5.X, plus any related Bloom.exe_Url directories.
Register for an earlier version of Bloom.
stepNotes: Verify the registration info has been saved.

src-tc359-r48.stepDescription: Corrupt collection file, open Bloom, test recovery and errors.
checklistSteps: Edit the collection file to intentionally corrupt its integrity.
Try navigating to the corrupt collection.
stepNotes: Verify error handling works.

src-tc12-r49.stepDescription: Go offline, report a problem, verify friendly guidance.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom provides a friendly solution.

src-tc360-r50.stepDescription: Verify error report details can be copied.
stepNotes: Verify the copied information is complete and usable.

src--r51.stepDescription: Report problem with unusual email characters.
checklistSteps: Try reporting a problem with an email address containing unusual characters.
stepNotes: BL-9995 has been put on hold; ignore that issue for now.

src-2190.stepDescription: Enable BloomSandbox test code, open sample books, verify errors.
checklistSteps: Use a Debug build, or set the BloomSandbox environment variable to yes.
Unzip the Fake Error Report Collection from Bloom Team/Testing/Bloompacks.
Open the books one by one.
stepNotes: A release build may still not work even with BloomSandbox set.
Verify the error messages match the screenshots.
BL-12226 is a known issue the team is willing to live with.

src-tc15-r53.stepDescription: Alt-Tab away from error dialog and keep it visible.
stepNotes: Verify the error dialog does not disappear from view and stays on top of Bloom.

src-2230.stepDescription: Measure performance and add all Basic Book page types.
checklistSteps: Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.
Create or open a Basic Book.

src-tc155-r185.stepDescription: Measure performance, change cover color in Web, verify propagation.
checklistSteps: Turn on Performance Measuring for all tests and monitor.
Change the cover color in Web.
stepNotes: Verify the color propagates to the BloomPUB preview.
Verify the new custom colors are added to the quick chooser.

src-tc400-r190.stepDescription: Retest uploads and verify only changed files re-upload.
checklistSteps: Run several upload tests with small and large file changes.
stepNotes: Verify only changed files are re-uploaded.
Expect PDFs and small regenerated files to upload again.

src-r187.stepDescription: Read template and verify icons for content types.
stepNotes: Verify icons appear for all content types.

src-tc78-r191.stepDescription: Upload and re-upload a book with more than 1000 files.
checklistSteps: Upload a book with more than 1000 files.
Add a page and an image, then re-upload.
stepNotes: Verify the upload succeeds without error.
Verify the re-upload skips unchanged .mp3 files.

src-tc17-r208.stepDescription: Install old Bloom, update it, then test BloomPUB variety.
checklistSteps: Install an older version of Bloom.
Test a variety of BloomPUBs from Blorg.
stepNotes: Verify the correct fonts are used.

src-tc79-r212.stepDescription: Reupload as same user, confirm update, verify new title.
checklistSteps: Edit the book and change its title.
When the collision dialog appears, choose Yes.
stepNotes: Verify the new title is displayed.

src-4050.stepDescription: Clean RTB residue, install RTB, verify DR and LR defaults.
checklistSteps: Clean up any residue so the computer appears unused for making the RTB.
stepNotes: Verify a new DR/LR sees the decodable and leveled reader settings, including sample words, allowed words, and texts.

src-4070.stepDescription: Make Decodable book from template and verify correct stage.
checklistSteps: Make a new Decodable book from one of the templates.
stepNotes: Verify it starts on the correct stage and shows the DR toolbox tool.

src-4090.stepDescription: Click format gear in DR and confirm it is blocked.
checklistSteps: In a Decodable Reader context, click the format gear.
stepNotes: Verify Bloom tells you that the action is not allowed.

src-4110.stepDescription: Check which Decodable stage new books inherit.
checklistSteps: Set a Decodable stage using the control.
Create a new book from the main DR template.
stepNotes: Verify the new book gets the stage last set with the control, not stage 1 and not merely the last stage seen in another book.
A book made from a bloompack template may use the stage defined by that template.

src-4150.stepDescription: Provide sample words using textbox, folder, and both.
checklistSteps: Provide sample words in Type words here only.
Provide sample words in the sample texts folder only.
Provide sample words using both sources with UTF content.

src-tc349-r455.stepDescription: Report error and include Allowed Words files correctly.
checklistSteps: Report an error for the specified book.
stepNotes: Verify the files under Allowed Words are included and that the subdirectory structure is respected.

src-tc290-r490.stepDescription: Re-record same passage and verify playback after each try.
checklistSteps: Re-record the same bit several times using different words.
After each recording, press the 4) Check button.
stepNotes: Confirm it plays back what you just recorded.

src-tc177-steps-21-to-33-r494.stepDescription: Record by whole textbox and verify sentence mode stays disabled.
checklistSteps: With By Whole Textbox selected, record something.
stepNotes: Verify By Sentence is disabled and the tooltip tells you to clear the existing recording first.

src-tc179-r497.stepDescription: Use formatted text and test sentence and full-page audio.
checklistSteps: Create text containing words with bold, color, and other formatting.
Test play one sentence at a time.
Test listen to the whole page.
stepNotes: This stresses Bloom's sentence finding, segment markers, and highlighting markup at the same time.

src-tc183-steps-32-to-41-r502.stepDescription: Import recording into split paragraph and note highlight behavior.
checklistSteps: Import a recording into a previously split paragraph.
stepNotes: Highlighting should be reset, but currently is not; this is being tolerated for now.

src-tc184-r503.stepDescription: Test Cover Page audio and reordered elements.
checklistSteps: Test audio on the Cover Page.
Test reordering elements, including the image description.

src-5062.stepDescription: Insert picture-only page, record title, verify Speak disabled.
checklistSteps: Insert a just a picture page and record its title.
stepNotes: Verify the 3) Speak button is disabled.

src-tc330-r530.stepDescription: Use By Whole Textbox and edit timing files.
checklistSteps: In the Advanced section, turn on By Whole Textbox.
From More, choose Edit Timings File and make changes.
From More, choose Apply Timings File and choose the file you edited.
stepNotes: Verify your timing changes are evident afterward.
